prompt:
In quiet testing mode with no active tasks, what should the system do next?
A. Spawn autonomous background tasks to gather more data.
B. Stay idle and wait for explicit user or evaluation input.
C. Re-enable pending startup backlog automatically.
D. Switch to the strong model for all future work.
Answer with only the letter.

What is the main purpose of the weak/strong bootstrap loop in this project?
A. Maximize GPU utilization during development.
B. Replace all evaluation with model self-judgment.
C. Use a stronger model to improve the system until the weak model can meaningfully improve it too.
D. Keep all models on the same provider for consistency.
Answer with only the letter.

Why is the repository intentionally structured into explicit modules, telemetry, and documentation?
A. To make it easier for small-context models to navigate and make better decisions.
B. To hide complexity from contributors.
C. To force all work through the UI.
D. To prevent local inference from being used.
Answer with only the letter.

Which event counts as secondary ignition in this project?
A. The strong model improves the harness once.
B. A weak-originated change is evaluated, promoted, and measurably improves weak-model performance.
C. Any benchmark finishes without errors.
D. The strong and weak models use the same provider.
Answer with only the letter.

If a harness change is promoted after evaluation, how should the system treat it?
A. Apply it only to the strong tier.
B. Apply it only to the weak tier.
C. Apply it as the active harness configuration for future evaluation runs, regardless of which tier proposed it.
D. Store it only in logs until a human copies it manually.
Answer with only the letter.
